description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7.5 agentic cluster: approval-layer exploitation (ASI09) vs gate placement, retrospective governance audit, content overreliance, and oversight tiering.

cases.prompt: Review whether our agent's approval prompts could be rubber-stamped or gamed — the operators mostly just read the agent's own explanation and click approve.
cases.reason: Attacking the approval layer's resilience (fatigue, self-report gap, deception) is ASI09; human-approval-boundary places the gates and agent-governance-audit checks compliance after the fact.

cases.prompt: Which agent actions must stop for explicit human approval — schema changes, production data, deploys, billing?
cases.reason: Defining the high-risk boundaries where work halts for approval is the Phase 1 gate skill; the trust reviewer assumes gates exist and tests whether they hold against manipulation.

cases.prompt: For last week's agent-driven change, verify from the PR timeline and approval records whether every required human approval actually happened.
cases.reason: Evidence-based post-hoc verification of process compliance is the governance audit; ASI09 is the design-time review of whether the approval mechanism resists exploitation.

cases.prompt: Users take our AI's confident answers at face value and act on them without verification — design controls against that overreliance.
cases.reason: Overreliance on AI CONTENT (grounding, citations, calibrated uncertainty, UX trust signals) is LLM09; ASI09 covers humans authorizing agent ACTIONS being fatigued or deceived.

cases.prompt: Classify this AI feature's risk tier and decide whether it needs human-in-the-loop or human-on-the-loop oversight.
cases.reason: Matching oversight level to impact tier is governance/risk posture; the trust reviewer hardens whatever oversight tier was chosen against fatigue and deception.

cases.prompt: Build the per-tool permission matrix and decide which tool calls are irreversible enough to need an approval trigger.
cases.reason: Per-tool blast radius and approval triggers are the LLM06/ASI02 tool boundary; the trust reviewer tests the human side of the gates those triggers fire.
